content_en: Then you shall compete in it as the people before you competed in it and it shall destroy you as it destroyed them." (Recorded by al-Bukhari and Muslim.) Furthermore, if a person is not using what Allah is giving him in the proper way, it may be a source of punishment for him and not a source of joy. He will be held responsible for all of the wealth that he gained and how he used it. It would be better for a person not to have wealth than to have wealth but to use it in an improper manner. The important question is who will enjoy the pleasures of the Hereafter. This is the concern of the true believers. The true believers, such as the Companions in this hadith, understand well the message of the following verses of the Quran directed toward those whose main concern is this life, ;t '' J ,,,. ,,,,,, ..,, J ,.,, " ,.,, J <> J T;'" ; ! ·. I ಸI / f ? i.,I @ -.?"' A .r:- B / ) C D ฏ(..)' B "You do not spend anything and desire by that Allah's pleasure except that you will be rewarded for it, even [the food] that you put in your wife's mouth."1 It seems that the reward for such permissible acts, such as fulfilling one's desire with one's wife, or obligatory acts, such as supporting one's family, is conditional upon the intention behind the act. If the intention is to do what is permissible instead of forbidden or to fulfill one's obligation as laid down by Allah, the act will be rewarded by Allah. However, if the act is performed with no such thoughts in one's mind, there may be no reward for the act and it would simply be a permissible act without any concurrent punishment or reward. This is ibn Rajab's and al-Haitami's conclusion and it seems to be supported by the particularizing hadith just mentioned above.2 lbn Rajab further supports his conclusion by referring to the verse in the Quran, мÙ jf Ƥ_,੊ jf ϗϘ ;r £ JN f;.J 2 .1ff=  Ǜ J / / ,, .... ǰ ,.,, ,,,. ,,, - ,,, ... ... .,. (_ - J 1;...1 9y Jj: "; 1I ..:;I/;,_;'.: ;1"੯1 C·? Ju.i 2j ifUI :£-! ,, ,, "There is no good in most of their secret talks save (in) him who orders charity or righteousness or conciliation between mankind; and he does this seeking the good pleasure of Allah. We shall give him a great reward" (al-Nisaa 1 14). In this verse, Allah refers to the act as good but restricts the reward by the condition that Allah's pleasure is sought by the act. This implies that the act would not be rewarded if there was no good intention or if the intention is bad. After making the above conclusion, ibn Rajab then presents the following interesting quote from Abu Sulaimaan al-Daaraani, "If a person does a good act without any intention in it, his intention to choose Islam over all other religions would be sufficient for him." In other words, the person will be rewarded even for those worldly acts without intention simply due to his willingly entering Islam and choosing to follow that proper way of life. Allah knows best. A Permissible Act may be Transformed into an Act Rewarded by Allah Al-Nawawi wrote, 1 Even though the maintaining of one's wife is obligatory upon the husband, if he fulfills that obligation seeking Allah's pleasure, he will be rewarded for it. 2 lbn Rajab, Jaami, vol. 2, p. 65; al-Haitami, Fath, p. 206. It is also al-Munaawi's conclusion in al-Munaawi, vol. 5, p. 2 1 . Commentary on the Forty Hadith of al-Nawawi [This hadith] contains evidence that permissible acts can become acts of obedience due to sound intentions. For example, sexual intercourse can become an act of worship if the person intended to fulfill the right of his wife and to treat her well as Allah has commanded him, or for the sake of seeking a pious son, or to keep himself chaste or his wife chaste, keeping them both from looking at what is forbidden, thinking about that or considering that or due to any other pious intention. 1 The same is true for eating, drinking and other permissible as well as necessary acts of life. If a person approaches them in the proper way, with the intention to do what is pure and legal and avoid what is forbidden, they may all be acts by which he may attain Allah's pleasure. Such is the great mercy of Allah that He may reward humans for doing what they need to do simply because they make sure that they do it in the proper, legal manner. Al-Haitami points out that such actions can even become acts of charity if their benefits accrue to others, as is the case described here.2 "They said, 'O Messenger of Allah, when one of us fulfills his desire, he will have a reward for that?' ...
content_id:
'Kemudian kalian akan bersaing di dalamnya sebagaimana orang-orang sebelum kalian bersaing dan hal itu akan menghancurkan kalian sebagaimana ia menghancurkan mereka.' (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim.) Selain itu, jika seseorang tidak menggunakan apa yang Allah berikan kepadanya dengan cara yang semestinya, itu bisa menjadi sumber hukuman baginya dan bukan sumber kebahagiaan. Beliau akan dimintai pertanggungjawaban atas semua kekayaan yang diperolehnya dan bagaimana beliau menggunakannya. Akan lebih baik bagi seseorang untuk tidak memiliki kekayaan daripada memiliki kekayaan tetapi menggunakannya dengan cara yang tidak semestinya.

Pertanyaan yang penting adalah siapa yang akan menikmati kesenangan-kesenangan akhirat. Inilah yang menjadi kepedulian orang-orang beriman yang sejati. Orang-orang beriman yang sejati, seperti para sahabat dalam hadis ini, memahami dengan baik pesan ayat-ayat Al-Quran berikut yang ditujukan kepada mereka yang kepedulian utamanya adalah kehidupan ini.

Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidaklah kamu membelanjakan sesuatu pun seraya mengharapkan ridha Allah kecuali kamu akan mendapat pahala karenanya, hingga (makanan) yang kamu suapkan ke mulut istrimu.' Tampaknya pahala untuk tindakan-tindakan yang diperbolehkan seperti itu, seperti memenuhi keinginan seseorang dengan istrinya, atau tindakan-tindakan wajib, seperti menafkahi keluarganya, tergantung pada niat di balik tindakan tersebut. Jika niatnya adalah melakukan yang diperbolehkan alih-alih yang dilarang atau untuk memenuhi kewajibannya sebagaimana ditetapkan oleh Allah, tindakan itu akan mendapat pahala dari Allah. Namun, jika tindakan itu dilakukan tanpa pikiran seperti itu, mungkin tidak ada pahala untuk tindakan itu dan itu hanya akan menjadi tindakan yang diperbolehkan tanpa hukuman atau pahala apa pun.

Ibn Rajab lebih lanjut mendukung kesimpulannya dengan merujuk pada ayat dalam Al-Quran: 'Tidak ada kebaikan pada kebanyakan bisikan-bisikan mereka, kecuali bisikan-bisikan dari orang yang menyuruh (manusia) memberi sedekah, atau berbuat ma'ruf, atau mengadakan perdamaian di antara manusia. Dan barangsiapa yang berbuat demikian karena mencari keredhaan Allah, maka kelak Kami memberi kepadanya pahala yang besar' (An-Nisa: 114). Dalam ayat ini, Allah menyebut tindakan itu sebagai kebaikan tetapi membatasi pahala dengan syarat bahwa ridha Allah dicari melalui tindakan tersebut. Ini menyiratkan bahwa tindakan itu tidak akan mendapat pahala jika tidak ada niat baik atau jika niatnya buruk.

Setelah membuat kesimpulan di atas, Ibn Rajab kemudian menyajikan kutipan menarik berikut dari Abu Sulaiman Al-Darani: 'Jika seseorang melakukan tindakan kebaikan tanpa niat apa pun di dalamnya, niatnya untuk memilih Islam di atas semua agama lain akan cukup baginya.' Dengan kata lain, seseorang akan mendapat pahala bahkan untuk tindakan-tindakan duniawi tanpa niat semata-mata karena ia dengan sukarela masuk Islam dan memilih untuk mengikuti cara hidup yang benar itu.

Tindakan yang Diperbolehkan Mungkin Diubah Menjadi Tindakan yang Mendapat Pahala dari Allah: Al-Nawawi menulis: 'Hadis ini mengandung bukti bahwa tindakan-tindakan yang diperbolehkan dapat menjadi tindakan ketaatan karena niat yang baik. Misalnya, hubungan suami istri dapat menjadi ibadah jika seseorang berniat untuk memenuhi hak istrinya dan memperlakukannya dengan baik sebagaimana Allah perintahkan kepadanya, atau demi mencari anak yang saleh, atau untuk menjaga kesucian dirinya atau istrinya, menjaga keduanya dari memandang apa yang dilarang, memikirkan atau mempertimbangkan hal itu, atau karena niat saleh lainnya.' Hal yang sama berlaku untuk makan, minum, dan tindakan-tindakan lain yang diperbolehkan maupun yang diperlukan dalam kehidupan. Jika seseorang mendekatinya dengan cara yang semestinya, dengan niat untuk melakukan yang murni dan halal serta menghindari yang dilarang, semuanya bisa menjadi tindakan yang dengannya beliau dapat meraih ridha Allah.
content_ar: Then you shall compete in it as the people قبل you competed in it and it shall destroy you as it destroyed them." (Recorded by البخاري and مسلم.) علاوة على ذلك, إذا a person is not using what الله is giving him in the proper الطريق, it may be a source of العقاب for him and not a source of joy. He will be held responsible for all of the الثروة that he gained and how he used it. It would be better for a person not to have الثروة than to have الثروة but to use it in an improper manner. The المهم question is who will enjoy the pleasures of الآخرة. This is the concern of the true المؤمنون. The true المؤمنون, مثل the الصحابة in this الحديث, understand well the message of the following الآيات of the القرآن الكريم directed toward those whose الرئيسي concern is this الحياة, ;t '' J ,,,. ,,,,,, ..,, J ,.,, " ,.,, J <> J T;'" ; ! ·. I ಸI / f ? i.,I @ -.?"' A .r:- B / ) C D ฏ(..)' B "You do not spend anything and desire by that الله's pleasure except that you will be rewarded for it, even [the food] that you put in your الزوجة's mouth."1 It seems that the الأجر for such permissible acts, مثل fulfilling one's desire with one's الزوجة, or obligatory acts, مثل supporting one's الأسرة, is conditional upon the intention behind the act. إذا the intention is to do what is permissible instead of forbidden or to fulfill one's الالتزام as laid down by الله, the act will be rewarded by الله. غير أن, إذا the act is performed with no such thoughts in one's العقل, there may be no الأجر for the act and it would simply be a permissible act without any concurrent العقاب or الأجر. This is ibn Rajab's and al-Haitami's conclusion and it seems to be supported by the particularizing الحديث just mentioned above.2 lbn Rajab further supports his conclusion by referring to the الآية in the القرآن الكريم, мÙ jf Ƥ_,੊ jf ϗϘ ;r £ JN f;.J 2 .1ff=  Ǜ J / / ,, .... ǰ ,.,, ,,,. ,,, - ,,, ... ... .,. (_ - J 1;...1 9y Jj: "; 1I ..:;I/;,_;'.: ;1"੯1 C·? Ju.i 2j ifUI :£-! ,, ,, "There is no الخير in most of their secret talks save (in) him who orders الصدقة or righteousness or conciliation between mankind; and he does this seeking the الخير pleasure of الله. We shall give him a great الأجر" (al-Nisaa 1 14). In this الآية, الله refers to the act as الخير but restricts the الأجر by the condition that الله's pleasure is sought by the act. This implies that the act would not be rewarded إذا there was no الخير intention or إذا the intention is bad. بعد making the above conclusion, ibn Rajab then presents the following interesting quote from Abu Sulaimaan al-Daaraani, "إذا a person does a الخير act without any intention in it, his intention to choose الإسلام over all other religions would be sufficient for him." بعبارة أخرى, the person will be rewarded even for those worldly acts without intention simply بسبب his willingly entering الإسلام and choosing للاتباع that proper الطريق of الحياة. الله knows best. A Permissible Act may be Transformed into an Act Rewarded by الله Al-Nawa
